title: Intro 2
description: This exercise introduces you to Rust's string formatting with println! and multiple placeholders.
instructions: Fix the println! macro to correctly format the output using the provided variables.
hints: The println! macro needs arguments to fill each `{}` placeholder.
You need to pass x and y as arguments after the format string.
The correct syntax is: println!("x is {} and y is {}", x, y);
explanation: In Rust's println! macro, each `{}` placeholder must have a corresponding argument. The arguments are matched to placeholders in order. You can also use named arguments like `{x}` directly if the variable name matches.
